title: Modes
content:
Claude Code has three operating modes. Cycle through them with **Shift + Tab**.

| Mode | Behaviour | When to use |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| **Ask (default)** | Asks for approval before every action | Normal work, when you want control |
| **Plan** | Creates a detailed plan before coding | New features, complex tasks, unclear scope |
| **Code (auto-accept)** | Executes without asking permission | When you trust the task and want speed |

**Best practice:** Always start complex tasks in Plan mode. Review the plan, then switch to Code mode to execute.

---

title: Key Shortcuts
content:
| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Shift + Tab` | Cycle between modes |
| `Esc Esc` | Clear current input |
| `Alt + Enter` (Win) / `Option + Enter` (Mac) | New line without submitting |
| `↑ / ↓ arrow` | Cycle through previous commands |
| `Ctrl + O` | Toggle verbose/log mode (see what Claude is thinking) |
| `Ctrl + B` / `Cmd + B` | Send current task to background |
| `Ctrl + T` | View background tasks |
| `Esc` | Interrupt/stop current execution |
| `?` | Show all shortcuts |

---

title: CLAUDE.md — Persistent Memory
content:
The `CLAUDE.md` file is read at the start of every session. It gives Claude persistent context across sessions.

**How to create it:** Run `/init` — Claude will analyse the codebase and generate a CLAUDE.md automatically. Edit it to add your specific rules.

**What to put in it:**
- Project identity and purpose
- Coding conventions or workflow rules
- Git commit instructions (e.g. "commit after every task, push daily")
- What to do and what to avoid

**Without CLAUDE.md:** Every new session starts blank. Claude won't remember rules, preferences, or context from the last session.

**Rule files in `.claude/rules/`** are also loaded automatically and extend CLAUDE.md with domain-specific behaviour.

---

title: Context Management
content:
- Sessions do not persist memory by default — everything resets when you exit
- CLAUDE.md and rule files are the mechanism for persistent context
- Use `/clear` or clear context after the plan phase to save tokens during execution
- Git history is also a source of context — Claude can read `git log` to understand what was done

**Token saving tip:** After the planning phase, clear context before executing. Claude only needs the plan going forward, not the whole brainstorm.

---

title: Git Workflow
content:
Claude can manage git automatically if instructed in CLAUDE.md.

**Recommended instruction to add to CLAUDE.md:**
> "After completing any task, commit the changes with a clean commit message and push to GitHub."

Claude will:
- Run `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` automatically
- Write descriptive commit messages
- Handle the full git workflow without being asked each time

**Setup:** `gh auth login` to connect the GitHub CLI. Then instruct Claude to `git init` and create a GitHub repo.

---

title: Background Tasks
content:
Background tasks are long-running operations (web servers, watchers) that would block the terminal.

- Claude automatically puts blocking operations in the background
- View running tasks: `Ctrl + T` or `/tasks`
- Kill a task: select it and press the kill option
- Run multiple Claude instances in parallel for independent tasks

---

title: Slash Commands
content:
Key commands:
- `/help` — full command list
- `/init` — generate CLAUDE.md from codebase analysis
- `/model` — switch model (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku)
- `/tasks` — view background tasks
- `/agents` — manage subagents
- `/clear` — clear context window
- `/exit` — end session

---

title: Planning Best Practice
content:
Before starting complex work:
1. Enter Plan mode (`Shift + Tab`)
2. Stream your requirements — be specific about what you want
3. Let Claude ask clarifying questions — answer them
4. Review the plan
5. Say "yes, proceed" — Claude will clear context and execute

**Key insight:** Be very specific about what you want. Vague prompts → vague results. Specific prompts → great results. One small task at a time beats one massive request.

---

title: Working in a Code Editor
content:
Run Claude Code inside VSCode or Cursor for the best experience:
- Open terminal in the editor (`Ctrl + backtick`)
- See file changes in real-time as Claude writes them
- Left sidebar shows file tree (`Ctrl + B`)
- Not required — but highly recommended for visibility

---

title: Models
content:
| Model | Use case | Cost |
|-------|----------|------|
| **Opus** | Complex tasks, tool-heavy work | Highest |
| **Sonnet** | Most tasks, good balance | Medium |
| **Haiku** | Quick answers, simple tasks | Lowest |

Switch with `/model`. For agency work, Sonnet is the daily driver. Use Opus for research-heavy or complex multi-step tasks.

---

title: Skills
content:
Skills are reusable multi-step workflows. Instead of creating a full agent, define a skill that Claude can run on demand.

- Created with slash commands (e.g. `/lead-audit`)
- Stored in `.claude/skills/`
- Claude reads the skill definition and executes the steps

Useful for repeatable agency tasks: monthly reports, lead audits, client updates.

title: Code Editors
content:
### Cursor
- **Recommended** as the best editor to pair with Claude Code
- VSCode fork with built-in AI features
- Use Claude Code inside the Cursor terminal for the best workflow
- Shows Claude's file changes in real-time in the sidebar
- Download: cursor.com


### VSCode
- Standard alternative to Cursor
- Works the same way — open terminal with `Ctrl + backtick`
- No native AI features but Claude Code handles that

**Both:** Open a project folder → open terminal inside the editor → run `claude`. This gives you file visibility while Claude works.

---

title: Voice / Dictation
content:
### Whisper Flow
- Dictation tool that transcribes speech to text
- Used to "stream thoughts" into Claude prompts rather than typing
- Useful for long, complex prompts — speak naturally, Claude gets the context
- Integrates with any text field

---

title: Version Control
content:
### Git
- Essential companion to Claude Code
- Use it to checkpoint Claude's work and revert if it makes mistakes
- Install before using Claude Code seriously
- Claude can install it for you: ask "install git for me"


### GitHub CLI (`gh`)
- Connects local git to GitHub repositories
- `gh auth login` — authenticates with GitHub
- Claude can create repos, push code, and manage branches through it
- Free to use; repos can be public or private


### GitHub (web)
- Private repo for the agency workspace
- Protects work from local machine failure
- Audit trail of everything Claude has done

---

title: AI Tools
content:
### Claude Code (CLI)
- Primary tool — the AI orchestrator
- Runs in terminal / PowerShell
- Requires Claude Pro, Max, Teams, or Enterprise subscription — or API key
- Not a desktop app — lives in the shell


### Claude Desktop App
- Simpler interface for beginners
- Supports coding mode, planning mode, GitHub integration
- Not as powerful as the CLI for serious work
- Good for one-off tasks or quick questions

---

title: Deployment and API Tools
content:
### Modal
- **Verdict: Critical tool for turning Claude workflows into live products**
- Deploy skills/prompts as public URL endpoints in minutes
- Free $5 credit on signup; claim up to $30 in additional credits
- Cost in practice: "Still at $4.50 after months of use" — Nick Saraev (extremely cheap)
- Supports: API endpoints, webhooks, scheduled tasks, web forms
- Integrates with Make, Zapier, n8n, Lindy for no-code automation
- Use case: Deploy a `/lead-audit` skill as a web form anyone can fill in → returns a PDF
- Claude handles the deployment automatically when instructed


### Netlify
- Free static website hosting
- Good for quick web app launches
- Easy deployment from local folder


### n8n / Make / Zapier
- No-code automation platforms
- Connect Modal-deployed endpoints via webhooks
- n8n: open-source, cheaper at scale
- Make: more expensive but easier UI
- Zapier: simple but expensive for volume

---

title: Subscription / Billing
content:
- Claude Pro: cheapest entry point
- Will run out of credits on heavy usage — not overcharged, just runs out
- Model choice affects cost: Haiku << Sonnet << Opus
- For production agency use, plan for regular top-ups or a higher tier

---

title: Stack Recommendations (from videos)
content:
| Tool | Purpose | Verdict |
|------|---------|---------|
| Cursor | Code editor | Best choice |
| Git | Version control | Essential |
| GitHub | Remote backup + audit trail | Essential |
| Whisper Flow | Voice dictation for prompts | Useful for long prompts |
| Claude Code CLI | AI orchestrator | Core tool |
| Claude Desktop | Simpler UI | Good for beginners/one-offs |

title: What MCP Is
content:
MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets Claude Code connect to external services — Gmail, Google Drive, Notion, GitHub, browsers, databases — and interact with them directly during tasks.

Without MCP: Claude can only read/write local files and run shell commands.
With MCP: Claude can read emails, access Drive files, update Notion, post to GitHub, etc.

---

title: How to Add an MCP Server
content:
1. Open Claude Code settings
2. Go to MCP Servers
3. Add the server (usually via OAuth or API key)
4. On first run, Claude will ask you to verify trust — review carefully before approving

Each MCP is treated like installing software. Only add from trusted providers.

---

title: Highly Recommended MCP (from research)
content:
### Chrome DevTools MCP
- **Verdict:** "I use this constantly, basically every day, many times" — Nick Saraev
- Lets Claude control a live Chrome browser in real-time
- Can: take screenshots, extract page text, click elements, navigate URLs
- Better quality than any native browser tool Claude provides
- Developed by the Anthropic team specifically — highest quality MCP available
- **Agency use:** Website QA, competitive research, AI visibility testing in browser

---

title: Current Agency MCP Targets
content:
### Gmail MCP
- **Purpose:** Alex reads the `alex@onlineoptimisers.net` inbox, summarises emails, drafts replies
- **Setup:** Claude Code → Settings → MCP Servers → Add Gmail → OAuth with alex@ account
- **Test:** Alex summarises the last 5 emails in the alex@ inbox
- **Status:** Pending — requires Google account creation first


### Google Drive MCP
- **Purpose:** Alex reads client briefs, writes deliverables, accesses Google Doc reports
- **Setup:** Same account OAuth as Gmail MCP
- **Permissions needed:** Read on client info folders, Read+Write on delivery and reports folders
- **Test:** Alex lists files in Client Delivery folder and reads one file
- **Status:** Pending — requires Google account creation first


### Google Sheets MCP (Optional)
- **Purpose:** Keyword tracking, reporting data, metrics spreadsheets
- **Setup:** Same account OAuth
- **Verdict:** Useful but not blocking — add after Gmail and Drive are working


### GitHub MCP (Optional)
- **Purpose:** Repository management, issue tracking, PR creation
- **Setup:** `gh auth login` is sufficient for most tasks without a full MCP
- **Verdict:** CLI tool handles most needs; full MCP adds value if managing multiple repos

---

title: Security Rules for MCPs
content:
- Never add MCPs from unknown sources
- Anthropic does not vet MCP servers — treat every new one with caution
- Finance folders are off-limits: no MCP should have access to business finances
- If an MCP request looks out of scope, stop and check with Donal
- Revoke access immediately if an MCP behaves unexpectedly

---

title: What MCPs Enable for the Agency
content:
| MCP | Agency Use Case |
|-----|----------------|
| Gmail | Inbox monitoring, draft client emails, follow-ups |
| Drive | Read client briefs, write deliverables directly to shared folders |
| Sheets | Auto-populate keyword reports, rank tracking data |
| Notion | If the team moves to Notion for SOPs or project management |

---

title: Broader MCP Ecosystem
content:
Other MCPs mentioned in research:
- **Browser/Playwright MCP** — automates web interactions (form fills, screenshots, scraping)
- **Slack MCP** — team communication
- **Linear/Jira** — project tracking
- **Postgres/SQLite** — direct database access

For the agency, Gmail + Drive + Sheets is the practical stack. Add others as real needs emerge.

title: Agents vs Skills — Key Distinction
content:
| | **Skills** | **Agents** |
|--|-----------|-----------|
| What | Reusable multi-step prompt workflows | Autonomous subprocesses that run independently |
| When | Repeatable tasks you run regularly | Complex parallel or long-running work |
| Setup | Markdown file in `.claude/skills/` | Full agent definition with tools and scope |
| Overhead | Low | Higher |
| For the agency now | Yes — use these | No — not yet (per CLAUDE.md rule) |

**Current rule:** Skills first. Agents only when a repeatable, autonomous workflow clearly justifies one.

---

title: Skills (Active Pattern)
content:
Skills are the right pattern for the agency right now. A skill is a markdown file that defines a repeatable multi-step process Claude can execute on demand.

**Examples for the agency:**
- `/lead-audit` — AI visibility audit for a prospect
- `/monthly-report` — generate monthly SEO report from client data
- `/client-update` — draft client communication from report data

**How they work:**
1. Create a markdown file in `.claude/skills/` describing the workflow
2. Invoke with `/skill-name` in the chat
3. Claude reads the skill definition and executes the steps

---

title: Parallel Execution
content:
Claude Code supports running multiple instances simultaneously:
- Multiple terminal/VS Code windows = multiple independent Claude instances
- Each instance operates on its own context
- All instances share the same filesystem
- **Risk:** Two instances writing the same file simultaneously = conflict

**Use case:** Run one Claude on client A's monthly report while another does a lead audit for a prospect.

**Practical rule:** Keep parallel instances working on separate files/folders to avoid conflicts.

---

title: Background Tasks
content:
Long-running operations can run in the background:
- `Ctrl + B` / `Cmd + B` — send current task to background
- `/tasks` — view all running background tasks
- Claude continues available for other work while background task runs

**Agency use:** Run a long research task in the background while drafting a client email in the foreground.

---

title: Worktrees (Advanced)
content:
Git worktrees allow Claude to work on an isolated copy of the repository. Changes don't affect the main branch until merged.

**Use when:**
- Testing changes that might break something
- Running experiments without risking the main workspace
- Multiple Claude instances working on the same repo simultaneously

Command pattern: `git worktree add ../workspace-experiment feature-branch`

---

title: Agent Teams (Future Pattern)
content:
When the agency scales, the specialist agent model makes sense:

```
Orchestrator (Alex)
├── SEO Delivery Agent — handles monthly reports and keyword work
├── Content Agent — drafts content from briefs
├── Sales Agent — runs lead audits and drafts proposals
└── QA Agent — reviews deliverables before send
```

**Not now.** Build this when each role has a proven, documented workflow.

---

title: Cost Warning — Agent Teams Are Expensive
content:
Agent teams consume tokens fast:
- Security audit on a large codebase: ~$80
- Complex multi-agent tasks can reach $1,000+
- Tokens spent are never refunded

**Rule:** Don't spawn agents without budgeting for the token cost. Skills are free by comparison. Use agents only when the task genuinely justifies it.

---

title: Debate Agents (Adversarial Pattern)
content:
Two agents argue against each other to improve quality:
- Agent A produces output → Agent B critiques it → Agent A responds → iterate
- Inspired by GAN (Generative Adversarial Network) architecture
- "Usually when you have two agents work adversarially against each other, the end result is higher quality"
- Best for: writing quality reviews, code reviews, strategic analysis

---

title: When to Create an Agent
content:
Only create a specialist agent when:
1. A workflow is fully documented as an SOP
2. That workflow runs at least weekly
3. The orchestrator is being bottlenecked by doing it manually
4. The agent can be tested and verified to produce quality output

Current status: 0 specialist agents. Focus is on proving the orchestrator workflow first.

title: Claude Code vs Traditional Automation Tools
content:
| Tool | Best for | Overlap with Claude Code |
|------|---------|------------------------|
| **n8n** | Visual workflow automation, API integrations | Some overlap on triggered workflows |
| **Make (Integromat)** | Multi-app automation, scheduled tasks | Claude Code handles logic; Make handles triggers |
| **Zapier** | Simple app-to-app automation | Claude Code is more powerful for complex tasks |
| **Claude Code** | AI-powered task execution, code generation | Replaces manual coding work, not simple triggers |

**Key insight from transcripts:** Claude Code doesn't replace n8n/Make/Zapier. They're complementary. Claude Code handles the AI reasoning and generation; automation platforms handle triggers and simple data routing.

---

title: Claude Code as an Automation Builder
content:
Claude Code can build automation workflows itself:
- Write n8n/Make workflow logic
- Generate code for custom integrations
- Build scripts that automate repetitive tasks
- Prototype and test automations faster than manual building

**Agency opportunity:** Use Claude Code to build automations for clients who need them, not just for internal use.

---

title: Practical Agency Automations to Build
content:
### Priority 1: Monthly Report Generation
- **Trigger:** Manual (Donal runs `/monthly-report` at start of each month)
- **Process:** Alex reads client data → drafts report → saves to deliverables folder
- **Tool:** Claude Code skill (`/monthly-report`)
- **Status:** SOP needed first


### Priority 2: Lead Audit Pipeline
- **Trigger:** Manual (Donal runs `/lead-audit` before a sales call)
- **Process:** Alex audits AI visibility → outputs 1-page summary
- **Tool:** Claude Code skill (`/lead-audit`)
- **Status:** Skill file needed


### Priority 3: Client Email Drafting
- **Trigger:** Monthly after report is approved
- **Process:** Alex reads approved report → drafts client update email
- **Tool:** Claude Code + Gmail MCP
- **Status:** Requires Gmail MCP setup


### Priority 4: Inbox Monitoring
- **Trigger:** Daily or as needed
- **Process:** Alex summarises unread emails in alex@ inbox → flags anything urgent
- **Tool:** Gmail MCP
- **Status:** Requires Gmail MCP setup

---

title: Skills → APIs Pattern (Modal)
content:
The most powerful pattern from the research: **turn a Claude skill into a live web endpoint.**

**How it works:**
1. Write a skill in `.claude/skills/` (e.g. `/lead-audit`)
2. Tell Claude: "Deploy this skill to Modal with a web form"
3. Claude creates the endpoint, form, and deploys automatically
4. Result: a live URL anyone can use

**Example — Lead Scraping as a Service:**
```
User visits: https://[account].modal.run/scrape-leads
Form: search query, location, number of results
Submission → Claude skill runs → Returns CSV download
Cost per run: ~$0.05–$0.50 (fractional cents at scale)
```

**Cost model:**
- Modal: $0.05–$0.50 per execution depending on complexity
- Free $5 credit to start; "$4.50 remaining after months of use"
- Can charge clients per-use or bundle into retainer

**Agency applications:**
- Lead audit form → returns 1-page PDF report
- Proposal generator → client fills brief → returns proposal HTML
- Monthly report runner → triggered by calendar → emails client
- Keyword research tool → fill search query → returns spreadsheet

**Integration pattern:**
```
Zapier/Make trigger → Modal endpoint (webhook) → Returns JSON →
Zapier parses → Saves to Google Drive / emails result / updates Sheets
```

---

title: Automation Principles
content:
1. **Automate what is already working** — don't automate a broken process
2. **Start with the highest-frequency tasks** — monthly reports, lead audits
3. **Human in the loop for sends** — Alex drafts, Donal approves and sends
4. **Document the automation** — if it breaks, you need to know how to fix it

---

title: Future Automation Ideas
content:
| Automation | Value | When |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Auto-generate competitor reports from search data | Sales leverage | After lead audit skill is proven |
| Weekly rank tracking digest from Google Sheets | Client retention | After Sheets MCP is live |
| Proposal generation from audit output | Faster sales | After AI ranking proposal template exists |
| New client onboarding checklist trigger | Consistency | After onboarding SOP is complete |

---

title: Notes from Research
content:
- Building automations with Claude Code is significantly faster than building them manually
- The barrier has dropped — non-coders can now build functional automations by describing what they want
- Most valuable use: automating the delivery operations of the agency so Donal's time goes to sales and client relationships

title: Revenue Opportunities from Claude Code Research
content:
### 1. AI Automation as a Service
- Businesses are still largely unaware of what Claude Code can automate
- Most SMBs have no AI-powered workflows — massive gap
- Sell automation builds the same way you sell SEO: monthly retainer
- Target: same ICP as current SEO clients (local service businesses)
- Package: assess → build → maintain → report


### 2. AI Visibility Audits as Lead Generation
- Offer free "AI visibility check" as the top-of-funnel hook
- Takes 15–20 minutes to run, produces a clear before/after picture
- Most local businesses will fail — the audit is the sales pitch
- Convert to AI ranking retainer or upsell existing SEO clients


### 3. Claude Code as an Agency Differentiator
- Competitors are not using AI at this depth for delivery
- Being genuinely AI-first (not just AI-branded) is a real advantage
- Speed of delivery, quality of insights, and depth of audits all improve
- Sell this as the reason why Online Optimisers gets better results


### 4. White-Label AI Automation for Other Agencies
- Agencies are starting to want AI capabilities they don't have in-house
- Online Optimisers could build and sell AI workflows to other agencies
- Similar to the current white-label SEO model but for AI
- **Caution:** Only pursue if retainer base is stable and bandwidth exists


### 5. Skills as Productized APIs
- Deploy Claude skills to Modal as public URL endpoints
- Clients interact via a simple web form — no technical knowledge needed
- Examples: lead scraping service, proposal generator, audit tool
- Revenue model: per-use fee or monthly access subscription
- Low marginal cost → high margin at scale


### 6. Training / Consulting on AI Implementation
- Teaching other business owners or agency operators how to use Claude Code
- Workshop, course, or 1:1 consulting format
- **Caution:** Time-intensive; pull from this only if content marketing is already working

---

title: Monetisation Signals from the Research
content:
- Automation builds that used to take hours now take minutes — the margin is significant
- Early movers in AI ranking have a real window before it gets crowded
- Local businesses are the last to adopt → least competition for AI services
- Combining local SEO + AI ranking = differentiated, defensible offer

---

title: Pricing Notes
content:
No specific pricing data extracted from the transcripts. Current agency pricing context:
- Current retainers: ~$1,400–$1,700/month average (based on $14k MRR / 9 clients)
- AI ranking upsell: additive to existing SEO retainer
- Target blended rate: higher than current to reach $100k/month

---

title: Ideas to Watch (Not Now)
content:
| Idea | Why Not Now | Revisit When |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| White-label AI for agencies | Bandwidth and complexity | Retainer base stable at 20+ clients |
| Training/consulting | Time sink | When content marketing is proven |
| New agency brand (localleadsoptimise.com) | No current benefit, adds cost | If product genuinely differs from OO brand |

title: Untitled
content:
# Mudi (Tasken.ai) — Automation & SOP Notes

Source: Tasken.ai — docs shared by Mudi (automations expert, friend)
Extracted: 2026-03-17

---

title: Source Files
content:
| File | Location | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Tasken.ai AIM Sheet.xlsx | `source-docs/Tasken.ai AIM Sheet.xlsx` | Not yet read — binary Excel, needs manual review |
| SOP Template (Notion) | `source-docs/SOP Template.html` | Extracted below |
| SOP Example: Technical SEO Audit (Notion) | `source-docs/SOP Example_ Technical SEO Audit & Prioritization.html` | Extracted below |

**Live Notion pages:**
- SOP Template: https://tasken.notion.site/SOP-Template-2049dfe6cf0b8094bd8fe12cf87431b9
- SOP Example (Tech SEO Audit): https://tasken.notion.site/SOP-Example-Technical-SEO-Audit-Prioritization-2049dfe6cf0b80c6b384fa1f3079a5a9

---

title: Key Insight: Mudi's SOP Structure
content:
Every SOP should have these 11 sections:

1. **Purpose** — what this SOP is for and why it exists
2. **Objectives** — 3–5 key goals (consistency, reduce back-and-forth, speed up QA)
3. **Scope & Frequency** — weekly/monthly/ad hoc, which clients, exceptions
4. **Roles & Responsibilities** — Task Owner / Reviewer / Support (optional)
5. **Tools Needed** — every platform, tool, data source, comms channel
6. **Folder & Naming Structure** — file naming conventions (YYYY-MM-DD_ClientName_TaskName_v1)
7. **Step-by-Step Process** — what to do / where / how to know it's done correctly
8. **Expected Outputs** — table: Output Type | Format | Quality Criteria
9. **Time Estimates (SLA)** — table: Step | Owner | Time Estimate
10. **Best Practices** — expert tips
11. **Quality Control Checklist** — pre-submission checklist (checkboxes)

**Optional add-ons:** Loom walkthroughs, mini SOPs for subprocesses, status tracking tables, version history

**Why this matters for us:** Our existing SOPs (`sops/`) are thin. This structure is the standard we should rebuild them to. Especially the QC checklist (directly feeds the `/test` skill) and the Expected Outputs table.

---

title: Technical SEO Audit SOP — Key Details
content:
**Mudi's tool stack for this SOP:**
- Project management: Monday.com (we use Asana — equivalent)
- SEO audit tool: **SE Ranking** (not Ahrefs/SEMrush — cheaper, worth evaluating)
- Output format: Google Sheets (tabs per issue category)
- Comms: Slack + Email

**7-step process:**

| Step | What | Time |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Initiate Audit | Create task in Monday.com | 5 min |
| 2. Prepare Google Sheet | Copy master template, rename per convention | 10 min |
| 3. Conduct SE Ranking Audit | Run crawl, wait for completion | 30–180 min |
| 4. Extract & Structure Data | Export from SE Ranking → populate Sheets, one tab per issue category | 60–120 min |
| 5. Intelligent URL Prioritization | Classify each URL as Target / Category / Other | 30–90 min |
| 6. Generate Executive Summary | Summary tab: priority breakdown + top issues | 15 min |
| 7. Update Tracking Log | Add entry to master tracker with link + timestamp | 5 min |
| **Total** | | **3–7 hours** |

**URL classification framework (directly usable for our work):**
- **Target Pages:** Homepage, service pages, collection pages, important product pages → highest priority fixes
- **Category Pages:** Blog, tag, archive pages → medium priority
- **Other Pages:** General content, utility pages → lowest priority

**Output format (table):**

| Output Type | Format | Quality Criteria |
|---|---|---|
| Audit Report | Google Sheet | Tabs per issue, accurate URL grouping, executive summary included |
| Prioritized URLs | Google Sheet | Every URL classified Target/Category/Other consistently |
| Tracking Log Entry | Google Sheet row | Audit name, completion timestamp, link to report |

**QC Checklist (for our `/test` skill):**
- [ ] All required tabs created in the audit report?
- [ ] URLs grouped correctly under each issue category?
- [ ] All URLs classified as Target, Category, or Other?
- [ ] Executive summary reflects the priority breakdown?
- [ ] Master tracker updated with correct link and timestamp?
- [ ] Naming conventions followed?

---

title: SOP Template Structure (Roles table format)
content:
| Role | Responsibilities |
|---|---|
| Task Owner | Executes the SOP, fills out documentation |
| Reviewer | Checks output, leaves feedback, approves |
| Support (optional) | Provides input, handles technical tasks |

---

title: What to Apply to Our Workspace
content:
| Insight | Action |
|---|---|
| 11-section SOP format | Rebuild `sops/seo/white-label-fulfilment.md` using this structure — it's currently a stub |
| QC checklist format | Use as the foundation for `.claude/skills/test.md` (tester skill) |
| URL prioritization (Target/Category/Other) | Add to our lead audit and SEO delivery process |
| Folder naming convention | Adopt `YYYY-MM-DD_ClientName_TaskName_v1` for all deliverables |
| SE Ranking as audit tool | Evaluate as a replacement or supplement to Ahrefs/SEMrush for technical audits |
| Expected Outputs table | Add to every SOP we write going forward |
| Time Estimates (SLA) table | Add to every SOP — helps set expectations with Niti/Meghana |

---

title: Untitled
content:
# ChatGPT Automation Ideas — Extracted Reference

Source: `source-docs/temp-paste` — ChatGPT conversation with deep business context (3 years of context)
Date extracted: 2026-03-18

This file captures the best and most original ideas from ChatGPT's automation brainstorm.
The HTML PDF and automation-master.csv are the primary deliverables — this is the raw reference so nothing gets lost.

---

title: Top 30 Best / Most Original Ideas
content:
### Lead Gen & Outreach

**1. Tech-Stack Detection for Outreach Angle**
Automatically detects what platform a prospect uses (WordPress / Webflow / Wix / Shopify) + whether they have forms, chat, analytics, conversion tracking installed. Uses this to customise the outreach angle — "we noticed you're on Wix and missing conversion tracking" hits differently than a generic pitch.
Tools: DataForSEO / BuiltWith API → Claude → Instantly personalisation field

**2. "Why Now" Trigger Finder**
Scrapes for signals that create a natural opening: recent bad reviews, new location opening, hiring ads (signals growth), site redesign, funding hints, ranking drops. Finds the moment a business is most receptive to help.
Tools: GBP API + SE Ranking + Google News API + LinkedIn → Claude → trigger tag in Airtable

**3. VSL Brief Generator Per Lead**
Instead of a generic cold email, generates a complete outreach asset pack per lead: personalised Loom script (what to show, what to say), list of specific screenshots to capture, 5 subject line options, 3 CTA variants.
Tools: Lead data + audit → Claude → brief in Drive → Loom recording guide

**4. Inbox Triage Classifier**
When outreach replies come in, Claude automatically classifies each as: positive interest / neutral question / objection (type) / unsubscribe request / spam risk. Routes accordingly — positive → Slack alert to Thonty, objection → Claude drafts reply, unsubscribe → GHL update.
Tools: Instantly webhook → n8n → Claude classify → route

**5. Multi-Channel Task Router**
If email bounces → automatically creates LinkedIn outreach task. If no LinkedIn found → creates phone call task. Never lets a qualified lead fall through because one channel failed.
Tools: Instantly bounce webhook → n8n → Airtable → Asana task

**6. Lead Recycling Workflow**
Leads that went cold 45-90 days ago get reactivated with a new trigger-based angle (different from original outreach). Uses any new public data point about the business as the hook.
Tools: Airtable date filter → DataForSEO/GBP refresh → Claude new angle → Instantly new sequence

---


### Proposals & Closing

**7. Discovery Call Summary + Proposal Builder (Minutes not Hours)**
Fathom transcript lands → Claude extracts: pains, budget signals, timeline, objections, specific phrases used → generates: proposal draft using their exact language, summary recap email to prospect, internal risk/opportunity notes. Done in under 5 minutes.
Tools: Fathom webhook → Claude → Google Doc proposal → Drive → Gmail draft

**8. Proposal QA Reviewer**
Before any proposal goes out: Claude checks it for mismatched scope (promising things not in retainer), overpromising on results, weak offer framing, missing pricing clarity, vague timelines. Returns pass/fix list.
Tools: Google Doc → Claude vs scope checklist → pass/fail → Slack

**9. Upsell Signal Detector**
Reads every sales call transcript and flags: moments where the prospect mentioned other problems we could solve, budget signals suggesting room for upsell, emotional pain points that map to our other services. Sends upsell brief to Donal.
Tools: Fathom → Claude → upsell opportunities tagged → Slack + Airtable

---


### Fulfillment & QA

**10. Delivery QA Reviewer Agent**
Any doc or deliverable marked "ready" → Claude runs it through a QA checklist: missing sections, weak claims, scope misalignment, formatting issues, placeholder text left in. Returns pass or fix list with specific issues.
Tools: Drive file → Claude vs SOP checklist → Slack pass/fail

**11. Churn-Risk Detector**
Monitors: slow progress on Asana tasks, low engagement from client (no replies, no feedback), missed reporting deadlines, stuck access requests. Flags at-risk clients before they churn.
Tools: Asana + Airtable + Gmail → n8n weekly → Claude score → Slack alert if risk > threshold

**12. SLA Watchdog**
Checks daily whether: outbound responses are overdue, reports are past send date, deliverables are past due date, access requests are unanswered. Escalates to Slack.
Tools: Asana + Airtable + n8n cron → Claude → Slack alert with overdue list

**13. Weekly Diagnosis + Self-Correction Loop**
Every week per client: pulls ranking data, site metrics, conversion data → Claude diagnoses what's working, what's stalling, competitive changes, fast wins → outputs P1/P2/P3 action list → executes top 3 → logs outcomes → feeds back into next week's diagnosis. Gets smarter per client over time.
Tools: SE Ranking + GA4 + GSC → Claude → Asana tasks → outcomes tracked → next diagnosis improved

---


### Meta Automations (Automations That Build Automations)

**14. SOP Writer from Repeated Tasks**
When the same task runs 3+ times in a week, Claude automatically: identifies the pattern, writes an SOP in Mudi's 11-section format, saves it to the workspace, creates a reusable prompt. Automations improving themselves over time.
Tools: Asana task log → n8n detect repeat → Claude → SOP file → git commit

**15. Agent Performance Review**
Weekly: tracks which automations are actually saving time vs creating overhead. Compares time invested in building vs time saved in operation. Suggests which to deprioritise or kill. Keeps the stack lean.
Tools: n8n execution logs → Claude → Slack weekly summary + Airtable scoring

**16. Error Log + Analysis**
n8n workflow failures logged automatically → Claude analyses patterns weekly: which workflows fail most, what types of errors, likely causes → suggests fixes → creates Asana task for each.
Tools: n8n error webhooks → Airtable → Claude weekly analysis → Asana fix tasks

**17. Task Routing Intelligence**
Learns over time which tasks work best as: full automation (no human), human-in-loop (human reviews output), human-only (too much nuance). Updates routing logic in n8n based on outcome data.
Tools: Outcome tracking → Claude → n8n workflow tag updates

---


### Content, Authority & YouTube

**18. Voice-Note → Full Video Pipeline**
Record a 2-5 minute voice note while driving or walking → n8n transcribes → Claude: cleans transcript, writes long-form video outline, 10 title options, thumbnail concept, 5 shorts cut points, LinkedIn post → all lands in content sheet. Thinking out loud becomes publishable content.
Tools: Otter.ai / Whisper API → Claude → Google Sheets content calendar

**19. Client Win Repurposer (1 Result → 8 Assets)**
A single client result → automatically becomes: website proof block, LinkedIn post, Facebook post, Instagram carousel, email to list, sales call asset, short proof clip script, quote card. One input, full content library.
Tools: Trigger (Airtable result logged) → Claude → 8 format variations → Buffer + Drive

**20. Case Study Machine**
Result screenshots + call notes → Claude generates: full website case study, sales PDF, LinkedIn post, short reel script, proof block. All formats from one source.
Tools: Screenshots + Fathom notes → Claude → Google Doc + Drive + Buffer

**21. Comment Mining Engine**
Pull YouTube/LinkedIn comments weekly → cluster by theme → Claude identifies: most common questions, objections, lead signals, future video ideas → outputs reply drafts + next-video topic ideas. Free market research loop.
Tools: YouTube Data API + LinkedIn API → n8n → Claude → Drive topics list

**22. YouTube Build-in-Public Engine**
Client win or new workflow built → Claude creates: video angle, full script, thumbnail concept, shorts breakdown, description, pinned comment, 3 platform cross-posts, email teaser. The agency documents its own work and builds authority automatically.
Tools: Trigger → Claude → full content pack → Drive + Buffer

**23. Workflow-to-Video Automation**
When a new n8n workflow is built and proven → Claude automatically generates: video angle (why this workflow matters), walkthrough script, thumbnail concept, shorts idea, LinkedIn post about the build. The tech becomes content.
Tools: GitHub commit hook → Claude → content pack → Drive

**24. Audience-Angle Splitter**
One core insight or result → Claude rewrites it for 6 different audiences: agency owners, local business owners, movers niche, real estate, marketers, Skool community. One idea, six targeted versions.
Tools: Core insight → Claude → 6 platform-specific versions → Buffer/Drive

---


### Niche-Specific Stacks

**25. Movers — Job → Content → SEO Flywheel**
After every completed move: auto-generate a blog post (city to city move summary), GBP post (we just moved a family from X to Y), case snippet (3 things that went well), FAQ addition (common question from that job). Every job becomes SEO and proof content.
Tools: Job completion trigger (GHL/CRM) → Claude → WP API + GBP API + Drive

**26. Movers — Peak Season Trigger System**
End of month / summer / start of school year → detect demand spike via Google Trends API or internal booking data → auto-generate: urgency landing page copy update, GBP post ("booking fast this weekend"), SMS blast to past customers ("moving soon?"), paid ads urgency copy.
Tools: Calendar + Google Trends API → Claude → WP + GBP + Twilio + Meta Ads

**27. Roofers — Storm / Weather Trigger**
Weather API detects significant storm in service area → within 2 hours: update website hero with storm damage messaging, create GBP post, send email to past customers, generate emergency landing page, push urgent Meta ad copy.
Tools: OpenWeatherMap API → n8n → Claude → WP + GBP + Meta Ads + Gmail

**28. Roofers — Insurance Claim Content Machine**
Generate educational content specifically around: how to file an insurance claim for roof damage, what your adjuster looks for, process timeline. This content captures high-intent search traffic that competitors ignore.
Tools: Claude → WP pages + blog posts + FAQ schema

**29. Local Partnership Builder (Movers)**
Identify complementary local businesses (realtors, storage facilities, junk removal, interior designers, painters) → Claude generates personalised partnership pitch → sends via Gmail with referral terms. Automated relationship building.
Tools: DataForSEO local search → Clay enrich → Claude pitch → Gmail draft

**30. HARO / Source Response Machine**
Monitors HARO / Connectively journalist queries → filters for relevance to local business, SEO, AI → Claude drafts expert response in Donal's voice → sends for approval → accepted placements stored → repurposed into trust assets on website.
Tools: Email parsing + n8n → Claude → Gmail draft → Drive placements log

---

title: Key Architecture Insights from ChatGPT Doc
content:
**Claude + n8n Hybrid Model:**
- Claude = brain (reasoning, writing, QA, decision-making)
- n8n = executor (triggers, routing, API calls, scheduling, state management)
- Human = reviewer (approves before sending, reviews quality gates)

**Files API for Context:**
Large reusable documents (SOPs, client profiles, templates) stored via Claude Files API — referenced across multiple workflow calls without re-uploading. Massive speed gain for recurring workflows.

**Prompt Caching:**
Same large system prompt cached for weekly/monthly recurring tasks (monthly report, weekly diagnosis). Reduces cost and latency for high-frequency, high-token workflows.

**Approval Gates via Slack:**
n8n routes draft outputs to Slack → team approves with emoji reaction → n8n publishes. Human oversight without manual execution.

**Stateless Pattern:**
Claude doesn't need to remember between calls — n8n manages state, stores outputs in Airtable/Sheets, passes context in each call. Reliable at scale.

---

title: Automation Categories Not Yet In Our Stack
content:
From the ChatGPT doc — ideas we haven't formally added yet:
- Rank-and-rent pipeline (own and rank local pages, rent leads to businesses)
- Owned media network builder (niche content sites as lead sources)
- Digital PR machine (automated journalist outreach, placement tracking)
- Podcast/interview repurposing (transcript → article + posts + clips + lead magnet)
- Local SEO myth-busting content series (debunking competitor false claims)
- Before/after website teardowns (video scripts + posts from competitor site analysis)
- City-page production line (auto-generate service pages per city at scale)
- Multi-location franchise management system (one Claude instance managing 10+ client locations)

title: Core Delivery Principles
content:
### Speed without quality loss
- Claude can handle first drafts, structure, and research — Donal reviews and sends
- The goal is to reduce the time between "task starts" and "output ready for review"
- One review cycle, not three — drafts should be 80%+ ready before human review


### Consistency beats perfection
- Use templates. Use SOPs. Use the same process every month.
- Clients value reliability and clarity over occasional brilliance
- Inconsistent delivery is the main cause of client churn


### Clients buy outcomes, not process
- Reports should lead with results and what they mean, not the methodology
- Avoid jargon in client-facing outputs
- Frame everything in terms of what it means for their business

---

title: Iteration Strategy (Key Insight from Research)
content:
Don't stop at the first output. The research showed a clear pattern:
1. Generate 3 different approaches/designs
2. Test or review them
3. Generate 3 more variants of the best one
4. Repeat

"Instead of just picking one option and stopping, explore all possible opportunities = 5x better output"

**For the agency:** When writing a proposal, report, or content brief — generate a v1, review it, then ask for 2 variations before picking. The second or third version is usually better than the first.

---

title: Report Quality Checklist
content:
Before sending any client deliverable:
- [ ] Data is real — no invented metrics, no placeholder claims
- [ ] Client-specific — references their business, their location, their services
- [ ] Results-first — headline numbers at the top, detail below
- [ ] Plain language — no SEO jargon unless the client understands it
- [ ] Next steps included — what are we doing next month and why
- [ ] Reviewed by Donal before sending

---

title: Communication Templates
content:
Use templates in `templates/emails/` for:
- Monthly update email (accompanies the report)
- Onboarding welcome sequence
- Upsell introduction

**Rule:** Personalise the template. A template that looks like a template loses trust.

---

title: Faster Delivery Using Claude
content:
**Monthly reports:**
1. Alex reads client profile + last month's data
2. Alex drafts report in Google Doc format
3. Donal reviews, adds personal notes, sends
4. Total Donal time: 10–15 min per client

**Lead audits:**
1. Alex runs AI visibility audit using `/lead-audit` skill
2. Output: 1-page summary with 3 biggest gaps
3. Donal uses in sales call
4. Total Donal prep time: 5 min

**Content briefs:**
1. Alex drafts from keyword data + client profile
2. White-label partner executes
3. Alex QAs output against brief
4. Total Donal time: minimal

---

title: Keeping Clients Happy
content:
Key drivers of client satisfaction in agency relationships:
1. **Communication** — proactive updates before they have to ask
2. **Clarity** — they understand what is being done and why
3. **Results** — actual movement in rankings, traffic, leads
4. **Consistency** — same quality, same timing, every month

What kills client relationships:
- Silence (no news = bad news in their mind)
- Jargon-heavy reports they can't understand
- Missed deliverable dates
- Promises not matched by outcomes

---

title: Upsell Strategy
content:
The best time to upsell is when:
- Results are strong (social proof moment)
- A new service directly solves a visible problem they have
- You're already in a monthly review conversation

**AI ranking upsell trigger:** During any monthly SEO review, run a quick AI visibility check live or in the report. Show them they don't appear in AI answers for their main service. That's the sales moment.

title: Untitled
content:
# JP Middleton — AI Aristotle Agency Model Analysis

Source: 3 YouTube video transcripts — `source-docs/3 x videos by JP Middleton on - ghl + ai - all in 1 biz model.md`
Date analysed: 2026-03-17
Images saved: `source-docs/jp-middleton-gap-diagram.png`, `source-docs/jp-middleton-hexagon-model.png`

---

title: Who Is JP Middleton?
content:
- Runs an "8-figure AI agency" — $25M+ in 7 years
- Scaled from $3k/month → $70k/month in 6 months early on
- Built the Allen platform (lead acquisition/nurturing): $10M charged to his clients who generated $56.7M in revenue
- Positioned as: "I didn't just build automations — I built a system that fixes the whole business"

---

title: The Core Business Model — "AI Aristotle Agency"
content:
> "The whole is greater than the sum of its parts." — Aristotle

**Key insight:** Competitors sell one component (ads, reviews, reactivation, AI receptionist). JP bundles all 6 into an end-to-end system. This creates atypical results → higher price → longer retention → harder to replace.

**Target market:** Brick-and-mortar local businesses — specifically **fitness franchises**

**Why fitness franchises:**
- Franchise advisory boards = after one win, 200+ location referrals
- Average location has 7,000+ contacts with no systematic follow-up
- High ROI sensitivity — already spending $5k+/month on ads
- Predictable pain points across all locations
- Franchise owners attend same events, same group chats = viral word of mouth

---

title: The 4 Gaps (Diagram 1 — Gap Model)
content:
Every small brick-and-mortar business has the same 4 gaps:

```
[GETTING LEADS] → [GETTING APPTS] → [CLOSING DEALS] → [CUSTOMER REFERRALS] → $$$$
```

- Competitors fix **one** gap
- JP's system fixes **all four simultaneously**


### What fills each gap:
| Gap | JP's Solution |
|---|---|
| Getting Leads | AI Lead Gen (paid ads + AI nurture) |
| Getting Appts | AI Receptionist + AI Re-engagement + Website Lead Nurture |
| Closing Deals | AI Sales Grader + AI Role Play Coach |
| Customer Referrals | AI Reviews + AI Referral System |

---

title: The 6 Components (Diagram 2 — Hexagon Model)
content:
### 1. AI Re-engagement Campaigns
- Pull inactive/opted-in contacts from database (CRM/GHL)
- Send personalised AI text offer
- AI books appointment for available slots
- Send appointment reminder
- **Result example:** 442 booked appointments, 163 showed up — zero ad spend


### 2. AI Reviews & Referrals
- Post-visit: send survey (rate 1-5)
- If 4-5 ★ → send Google review link
- If <4 ★ → ask for feedback (protect reputation)
- Post-review: send referral link with incentive (e.g. "win a free year's membership")
- **Result example:** 211 Google reviews + 82 referral leads generated automatically


### 3. AI Receptionist (+ Website Lead Nurture)
- Missed call → AI follow-up within 60 seconds
- Website form submit → AI response within 90 seconds (industry avg: 42 hours)
- Nurtures to book, sends reminders, handles no-show rescheduling
- **Data:** 400%+ increase in conversion when follow-up is within 5 minutes


### 4. AI Lead Generation
- Facebook/Instagram paid ads (feed placements only)
- Lead submits form → AI immediately nurtures → books appointment
- Combined with above systems = much higher conversion on same ad spend


### 5. AI Sales Grader
- All sales consultations recorded
- AI grades call against sales script (discovery, objection handling, close)
- Identifies specific rep weaknesses
- Manager gets targeted coaching report
- **Data:** 70% of reps get no formal training; those who do see 50%+ sales increase


### 6. AI Role Play Coach
- Rep gets custom role-play scenarios based on their weak areas
- Practices objection handling, follow-up, closing with AI
- Reps improve before they're in front of a real prospect

content:

| Offer structure | Bundle only — never sold as individual components |
| Pricing example | $12,000+/month (16-gym franchise owner) |

| Positioning | "We fix the whole system. Competitors fix one leak." |
| Sales proof | Gym client with JP's system: 25% booking → 50% booking, 25% show → 60% show = 5x conversions on same ad spend |

title: JP's Key Principles
content:
### From Video 2 — System vs Single Automations
- Selling individual automations = commoditized, low-margin, high churn
- Selling the integrated system = atypical results = higher price, 3+ year retention
- The competitor doing $150k/month (106 franchise locations) is ONLY running ads — one gap
- JP closes against them by solving all 4 gaps


### From Video 3 — Don't Build for Building's Sake
- Find a real business problem first
- Solve it manually → then automate
- Agentic workflows for their own sake = wasted time
- AI n8n workflows, Claude Code, Cloudbot etc are tools — only valuable when solving a defined problem

title: Automation Ideas Extracted (for automation sheet)
content:
All 6 components map directly to buildable n8n + GHL + Claude automations:

| # | Automation | Tool Flow |
|---|---|---|
| JP-1 | Database reactivation | GHL contacts → Claude segment by recency → Twilio SMS sequence → book → Airtable |
| JP-2 | Post-visit review ask | GHL post-visit trigger → Claude → Twilio SMS → if 4-5★ → Google review link |
| JP-3 | Referral sequence | Post-review → Claude → referral SMS with incentive → friend enters GHL system |
| JP-4 | AI auto-respond to reviews | GBP new review webhook → Claude draft reply → GBP API post |
| JP-5 | Missed call callback | GHL missed call → Bland.ai/Vapi call within 60s → qualify + book → Airtable log |
| JP-6 | Website form → instant SMS | Form submit → Twilio SMS in 90s → Claude nurture sequence → book appointment |
| JP-7 | Sales call grader | Fathom webhook → n8n → Claude grade 1-10 + strengths + improvements → Slack to Thonty |
| JP-8 | Role-play generator | Weak area identified → Claude → 5 tailored role-play scenarios → Slack/email |

title: Applicability to Online Optimisers
content:
### Direct parallels:
- **Reactivation** → run for moving/roofing clients with existing customer lists → pure upsell
- **Reviews + Referrals** → immediate add-on to any local SEO retainer
- **Missed call + website form** → AI receptionist as a standalone product
- **Sales call grader** → use internally for Thonty's own calls first (no client needed)
- **Role-play coach** → build Thonty's close rate before scaling outreach


### Bundle opportunity:
JP's model is exactly what the "AI ranking" offer could grow into — not just SEO visibility, but a full local business AI infrastructure. The SEO gets them to your door; the AI system is why they stay for 3+ years.


### Niche note:
JP focuses on fitness. Our strongest niche is moving companies + roofing. Same model applies:
- Moving company with 3,000 past customers → reactivation = booked moves without ads
- Roofing company after storm → reactivation + review ask = 50+ reviews in 2 weeks
